message:
fix(http): add HTTP/1.0 keep-alive support and handler-requested close detection

The HTTP/1.1 server did not handle HTTP/1.0 connection persistence
correctly: it never sent Connection: keep-alive for HTTP/1.0 requests
that should persist, and it ignored Connection: close headers set by
request handlers.

Add three new helpers:
- add_connection_keep_alive(): sets Connection: keep-alive header
- response_requests_close(): detects handler-set Connection: close
- finalize_response_persistence(): unified function that normalizes
  response version to match request version, detects handler-requested
  close, adds appropriate Connection header (close or keep-alive),
  and returns the final close-after decision

Apply finalize_response_persistence() to all three response paths:
- 417 Expectation Failed rejection (was only adding close)
- 100 Continue interim response (had no connection header at all)
- Normal handler response (was only conditionally adding close)

The 100 Continue response now gets proper persistence headers, and
HTTP/1.0 keep-alive responses correctly include Connection: keep-alive.

Add unit tests for all new helpers and integration scenarios:
- add_connection_keep_alive with and without existing header
- finalize_response_persistence for HTTP/1.0 keep-alive and close
- finalize_response_persistence preserving handler-requested close

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
